Think of it as the window to your vision—it plays a critical role in focusing light so you can see clearly. Because it is the eye's first line of defense against dirt, germs, and UV light, it is susceptible to various injuries, infections, and diseases. Squint, medically known as Strabismus, is a condition where the eyes do not align properly. While one eye focuses on an object, the other may turn inward, outward, upward, or downward. This misalignment can be constant or intermittent, affecting both children and adults. Beyond the cosmetic concern, untreated squint can lead to permanent vision loss (Amblyopia or “Lazy Eye”) and a loss of
